title: Build an ElevenLabs Voice Bot
tagline: Turn text replies into spoken audio with ElevenLabs.
description: You'll connect to the ElevenLabs API, pick a voice, generate speech from text, and combine it with an AI-generated reply so your chatbot can talk back.

steps.title: Set up your ElevenLabs API key
steps.instructions: Create a variable ELEVENLABS_API_KEY with a placeholder string value.
steps.criteria: User defines a variable named ELEVENLABS_API_KEY assigned to a string.

steps.title: Initialize the ElevenLabs client
steps.instructions: Create a client object for ElevenLabs using your API key.
steps.criteria: User creates a client object, passing the API key, e.g. ElevenLabs(api_key=ELEVENLABS_API_KEY).
steps.starter_code:
from elevenlabs.client import ElevenLabs

client = ElevenLabs(api_key=ELEVENLABS_API_KEY)

steps.title: Choose a voice
steps.instructions: Pick a voice ID (a placeholder string is fine) and store it in a variable. Add a comment on why voice choice matters for user experience.
steps.criteria: User defines a voice_id variable (string) with a comment explaining its relevance to UX/persona.
steps.starter_code: voice_id = "voice-id-placeholder"  # choose a voice that matches your bot's persona

steps.title: Generate speech from text
steps.instructions: Call the text-to-speech endpoint with some sample text and the chosen voice.
steps.criteria: User calls a text-to-speech generation method (e.g. client.text_to_speech.convert or client.generate) passing text and voice_id.
steps.starter_code:
audio = client.text_to_speech.convert(
    voice_id=voice_id,
    text="Hello! Thanks for calling, how can I help you today?"
)

steps.title: Save the audio output
steps.instructions: Write the generated audio bytes to a file (e.g. response.mp3).
steps.criteria: User opens a file in binary write mode and writes the audio data (iterating chunks if it's a generator).

steps.title: Connect it to your chatbot reply
steps.instructions: Take a text variable representing your chatbot's reply (from the chatbot project) and pass it into the speech generation call instead of a hardcoded string.
steps.criteria: User uses a variable (not a hardcoded literal) as the 'text' argument to the TTS call.
steps.starter_code:
bot_reply = "We're open until 9pm today."

audio = client.text_to_speech.convert(
    voice_id=voice_id,
    text=bot_reply
)

steps.title: Handle errors gracefully
steps.instructions: Wrap the TTS call in try/except so a failed voice generation doesn't crash your whole bot — fall back to text-only.
steps.criteria: User wraps the TTS call in try/except with a fallback (e.g. printing text, or setting audio = None).

steps.title: Control voice settings
steps.instructions: Add voice settings (stability and similarity_boost) to your call and comment on what each does.
steps.criteria: User adds a voice_settings object/dict with stability and similarity_boost fields, with comments explaining each.

steps.title: Stream audio instead of saving
steps.instructions: Instead of saving to a file, write a function that streams audio chunks directly (e.g. to a player or response stream).
steps.criteria: User writes a function/generator that yields or processes audio chunks without first writing to disk.

steps.title: Wrap it up
steps.instructions: Combine everything: a chatbot reply variable, error-handled TTS generation with voice settings, and either saving or streaming.
steps.criteria: User combines the pieces from previous steps into one coherent script with bot_reply, voice settings, error handling, and audio output.
steps.starter_code: # Paste your combined voice bot script here
